text: Text classification is one of the basic tasks in Natural Language Processing (NLP). Many text classification algorithms rely heavily on pre-defined features, such as dictionary, knowledge base, and tree kernels. In this work, we investigate a recurrent convolutional neural network (RCNN) for text classification without using any pre-defined features. The RCNN model learns local and global features for each word and then uses a recurrent structure to gather contextual information within a larger span, in contrast to the fixed window size of other neural network approaches and thus potentially with less noise introduced. To extract more important features of words, a max-pooling layer is used. Experiments are done on four benchmark datasets for text classification, and the experimental results show that our proposed model has achieved a new state-of-the-art performance on these datasets, with large improvements on the document-level datasets.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
